mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, generating negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals a breaking labor market that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and expansion in retail sales indicates a structural shift in consumer demand away from real estate toward goods, forecasting negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days as the housing cycle peaks.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods where HOUST declines and RSAFS rises concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mechanism produces negative 20-day returns in >70% of observations during early-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of accelerated monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0 percent reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion that directly supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns are negative in >55% of quarters with GDP growth above 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production falls below its 12-month moving average, it signals deteriorating manufacturing activity and weakening economic momentum, producing negative forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where INDPRO crosses below its 12-month moving average.
